Patch Set 2:

I'm fine with this change set. It don't break Xeon Phi compilation. But what about similar patch http://review.whamcloud.com/4822 (LU-2488)? I don't like that many code of those patches are duplicates. Could we join those patches in one?

Patch Set 2:

Why do you think that they are similar? Different algos - different implementation.

Patch Set 2:

I'm talking about service code. Look in inst.h and begin of crc16_t10_asm.S. They are pretty the same.

Patch Set 2:

inst.h was taken from the linux kernel. It is a set of assembly macros for older gcc versions. It is true this file could be shared with other crypto assembly routines.

Patch Set 2: Looks good to me, but someone else must approve

may we use external assembler (other then GCC) for it? because it's don't compatible with clang and C standard.

Patch Set 10:

Sure Alexey we can make it clang compatible as well. I'm surprised that clang lacks a gas compatible mode. Mind you I not familiar with clang assembly format.

Patch Set 10:

I tried it with the clang compile and didn't seem to have a problem. Can you post the errors you get with the clang compiler to the JIRA ticket.

parents.subject: LU-4007 libcfs: fix for removing lcw at lcw_dispatch_main

commit.subject: LU-3570 libcfs: accelerate crc32c with pclmulqdq
commit.message:
LU-3570 libcfs: accelerate crc32c with pclmulqdq

Using hardware provided PCLMULQDQ instruction to accelerate
CRC32C check sum. This instruction is present starting with
Intel Westmere and AMD Bulldozer CPUs.

Also reorganize assembler code of other PCLMUL inmplementation
with using common assembler macros from inst.h.

Signed-off-by: James Simmons <uja.ornl@gmail.com>
Signed-off-by: Dmitry Eremin <dmitry.eremin@intel.com>
Change-Id: I2099ff8af3591b3e2267cb30486d7a4f0a3e90c0
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/6927
Tested-by: Jenkins
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
